title: TinySA Flasher public application contract v2

contractVersion.const: Version of this JSON document shape and interpretation; increment for an incompatible serialization or semantic change.

applicationContractVersion.const: Version of the externally observable updater ownership, workflow, and safety guarantees; increment when a consumer must change integration behavior.

deviceContractVersion.const: Version of physical CDC admission, preflight, DFU write, and post-write verification assumptions and guarantees; increment when supported device behavior changes incompatibly.

releaseVersioning.const: OEM firmware releases are independently versioned immutable manifests; changing only the selected OEM release does not increment the three contract versions.

atomizerOperationalSession.const: Atomizer exclusively owns CDC analyzer/generator operation outside a firmware-update session.

flasherUpdateSession.const: TinySA Flasher exclusively owns CDC discovery/preflight, DFU admission/write, and CDC post-write verification for the complete firmware-update session.

mutualExclusion.const: Atomizer and TinySA Flasher must never access the same physical device simultaneously; close or disconnect Atomizer before starting an update session.

oem.const: The canonical immutable OEM release remains the default target and is fetched over an untrusted transport then admitted by exact size and SHA-256.

localCustom.const: A native-main file picker may admit a manifest-v1 ZS407 build and adjacent BIN into owner-only content-addressed storage after strict provenance, vector, embedded identity, size, and SHA-256 validation.

rendererPathPolicy.const: The renderer can request native target selection but cannot supply or observe a filesystem path.

targetChangePolicy.const: A target cannot change after preparation; a recovered prepared custom target must be rebound from its deterministic app-owned paths before write.

customQualificationProof.const: qualificationEvidenceSha256 is required exactly for qualified local-custom targets and forbidden for unqualified targets; native confirmation displays the custom manifest SHA-256.

customObservationPolicy.const: Ordinary CDC observation of matching custom version/revision labels cannot prove exact target bytes or provenance and remains custom-current/install-custom; only a just-completed durable write transaction may retain exact-target completion evidence.

descriptorWriteBoundary.const: The write path opens and verifies one regular-file descriptor, asserts that same open file description is stable immediately before spawn, and inherits it into dfu-util without resolving a firmware pathname.
